title: trichilia moschata subsp. matudae
intro: Trichilia moschata subsp. matudae, also known as the Mexican mastic tree, is a plant species in the family Meliaceae. It has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its seeds and leaves, which contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems, due to its potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and improving respiratory function. In herbal preparations, the seeds and leaves of Trichilia moschata subsp. matudae can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune system function and alleviate symptoms of certain health conditions.
